tldr: Checkmarx is an enterprise SAST, SCA, IaC, and API security platform across many languages, while Appknox specialises in mobile application security testing for Android and iOS. The switch narrows scope significantly, so it only makes sense if mobile is the dominant or sole concern.
why_switch: Refocus on mobile-only security and reduce broader SAST overhead
Move from custom enterprise pricing to a more focused subscription
Simplify the toolset around mobile binaries
Reduce the integration surface compared with a wide-scope SAST
what_to_export: List of Checkmarx projects and the repositories they cover
Open and historical findings with severity
export_method: Use Checkmarx's built-in export to pull findings as SARIF where supported; this is the best generic format for downstream tools. Document the project-to-repository mapping and any custom queries or rule overrides since these are highly tool-specific and will not transfer. Capture suppression and risk-acceptance decisions in a structured list so they can be re-applied. Archive historical reports for audit retention before cancelling the contract — proprietary scan history typically cannot be re-imported.
import_method: Onboard each mobile app to Appknox by uploading APK/IPA builds or wiring the build pipeline to push artifacts to Appknox after each build. Appknox scopes itself to mobile apps, so any non-mobile Checkmarx coverage (web app SAST, IaC, API scanning) will not have a destination and must be handled by another tool or accepted as a coverage gap. Re-apply suppressions manually based on the exported list, and configure user roles and notifications to mirror the Checkmarx setup. Run both tools on mobile builds in parallel until severity tuning and reporting are validated.

concept_mapping.note: Only mobile projects map; non-mobile projects do not

gotchas: Appknox is mobile-only — non-mobile Checkmarx coverage must be replaced separately or dropped intentionally
Custom Checkmarx queries do not port; you lose any tuning embedded in them
Historical scan data typically stays in Checkmarx; export reports for audit before cancellation
Enterprise contract terms may impose notice periods — check before announcing a cutover date
Severity scoring and naming differ; map them explicitly before tuning gates
Appknox runs against built binaries, so build pipelines need to publish artifacts reliably

email.subject: Refocusing our application security on mobile with Appknox
email.body:
Hi team,

Over the next 14 days we are narrowing our application security tooling and moving mobile coverage from Checkmarx to Appknox. Non-mobile coverage that lived in Checkmarx will be addressed separately, and we will note where there are temporary gaps. Both tools will run on mobile builds in parallel while we validate findings. Please flag any open Checkmarx item you want carried over.

Thanks for your patience.

Your customer
